summary: Good dependency health with well-maintained ecosystem libraries. Most direct dependencies are recent and from established projects (ropey, tree-sitter, crossterm, ratatui). Uses semantic versioning with range pins (e.g., 1, 0.28) rather than exact pins, relying on Cargo.lock for reproducibility. All dependencies are from the official Rust registry with no abandoned packages detected.

duplicates.recommendation: Not true duplicates - crossterm wraps mio for terminal handling; parking_lot for synchronization. Stack is well-layered.
Two versions of rustix - used by different dependency chains. Cargo handles this correctly; manageable.
Two versions used by indexmap and lru. Normal dependency resolution; no conflict.

supply_chain_risks.note: Small adoption base but used deliberately as Alfred's Lisp engine. Acceptable risk due to intentional selection.
Tree-sitter bindings use native C libraries compiled via cc crate. Standard practice, properly maintained.

recommendations.title: Upgrade streaming-iterator if still needed
recommendations.description: streaming-iterator 0.1.9 is from 2020. Verify if it's still necessary for alfred-syntax. Consider replacing with standard iterators if the streaming pattern is no longer critical.

recommendations.title: Add SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation
recommendations.description: Use cargo-sbom or similar to generate SBOM for supply chain transparency and security auditing.

recommendations.title: Add dependabot or renovate config
recommendations.description: Implement automated dependency update tracking for security patches even if major version bumps are deferred.

recommendations.title: Document dependency decisions
recommendations.description: Create DEPENDENCIES.md explaining why specific versions/crates were chosen (e.g., rust_lisp over alternatives).
